title: End if-else chains with else clause
description: If-else if constructs without a final else clause can lead to unhandled cases and defensive programming issues. A final else clause ensures all possible conditions are explicitly addressed or documented.
recommendation: Add a final else clause that handles the default case or contains appropriate comments.

noncompliant.description: If-else chains without final else clauses can leave cases unhandled, leading to undefined behavior.

compliant.description: Final else clauses ensure all cases are handled, providing predictable behavior and better error handling.

checks.message: Rule: End if-else chains with else clause
